---
title: "Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan"
chapter_id: "open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan"
part_id: "evidence-implementation-living-book"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "alignment_field"
  - "scf"
  - "planforge"
  - "vcm_public"
  - "spinoza"
  - "talos"
  - "rmi"
  - "cgs"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "moecot"
  - "coilmoecot"
  - "road_to_agi"
  - "vcm_editable"
  - "field_of_god_ai_constitution"
  - "project_theseus_whitepaper"
  - "circle_calculus_core"
  - "circle_ai_contract_suite"
  - "theseus_circle_transfer"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Source notes exist for all assigned sources, but claim-level source mapping remains incomplete."
  - "The research_backlog_record fixture validates schema/example consistency only; external literature normalization, direct citation checks, and new-paper triage rehearsals remain incomplete."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

# Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan` |
| Part | Part IV - Evidence, Implementation, and the Living Book |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `verification_bandwidth`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `scf`, `planforge`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `talos`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `genesiscode`, `simulation_scaling`, `moecot`, `coilmoecot`, `road_to_agi`, `vcm_editable`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `verification_bandwidth`, `benchmaxxing`; supporting: `alignment_field`, `scf`, `planforge`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `talos`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `genesiscode`, `simulation_scaling`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer`; connector/recovery: `moecot`, `coilmoecot`, `road_to_agi`, `vcm_editable` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `verification_bandwidth`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `scf`, `planforge`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `talos`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `genesiscode`, `simulation_scaling`, `moecot`, `coilmoecot`, `road_to_agi`, `vcm_editable`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer`; raw cache: `verification_bandwidth`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `scf`, `planforge`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `talos`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `genesiscode`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot`, `road_to_agi`; connector/recovery: `moecot`, `vcm_editable` |
| Test state | Source inventory validation, source-note validation, and `research_backlog_record.valid.json` protocol fixture validation pass; new-paper triage scenario remains planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter manages future work. It does not cite unread sources, infer bibliography metadata, or promote missing/private artifacts as evidence.

## Problem

A living book can decay into a pile of sources unless it has a research backlog. Future writing agents need to know which source to load, which chapter owns the boundary, which evidence is missing, which proof target is operational, and when a new paper should update an existing chapter instead of creating a duplicate one.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

A pile of sources or ad hoc citations does not tell future writing agents what to load, compare, prove, test, or defer. It also creates citation drift: a chapter may cite a source it has not actually mined, or a new source may be treated as evidence before it has an inventory record and source note.

## Core Claim

[open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] The bibliography and research agenda should map source families, external literature queues, missing papers, proof targets, experiment backlogs, and chapter insertion rules.

The claim remains at `argument` support. The repository has source inventory and source notes, but external literature normalization and claim-to-source mapping remain incomplete.

## Mechanism

The mechanism is a Research Backlog Record.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["New source or gap"] --> B["Inventory record"]
  B --> C["Source note / access status"]
  C --> D{"Existing boundary?"}
  D -- "yes" --> E["Update chapter source queue"]
  D -- "no" --> F["Propose precise chapter"]
  E --> G["Claim/evidence mapping"]
  F --> G
  G --> H["Proof/test/backlog item"]
```

The record should track source id, access state, assigned chapters, source-note state, claim-mapping state, external-literature need, proof/test backlog, insertion decision, and residuals.

## Interfaces

The interface is the Research Backlog Record.

Minimum fields:

- `backlog_id`
- `source_or_gap`
- `access_state`
- `assigned_chapters`
- `source_note_state`
- `claim_mapping_state`
- `external_literature_need`
- `proof_or_test_backlog`
- `insertion_decision`
- `residuals`
- `next_action`

Appendix G lists the corpus and external literature queue. Source notes support drafting. `docs/book_outline.md` tells future agents what to mine. `book_structure.json` prevents chapter-order drift. Appendix C records support-state boundaries.

## Invariants

- Do not cite unread sources as support.
- Do not infer bibliographic metadata.
- New papers update inventory before chapter claims.
- Missing sources are backlog items, not evidence.
- New chapters are added only when they own a distinct boundary.

## Failure modes

- Citation drift.
- Duplicate chapter creation for overlapping ideas.
- Missing sources treated as evidence.
- Private empirical results leaking into public claims.
- External references named by source documents becoming citations before direct verification.

## Minimal implementation

The smallest useful implementation is a research backlog schema and fixture. The current fixture makes future source ingestion and chapter insertion reviewable, but it does not normalize external literature or complete claim-to-source mapping.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Research backlog record fixture validation | Validate that a backlog record names source/gap, access state, assigned chapters, source-note state, claim mapping state, external-literature need, proof/test backlog, insertion decision, residuals, and next action. | implemented; passing via `python3 scripts/validate_protocol_examples.py` |
| Source inventory validation test | Check that assigned source IDs exist before chapter claims use them. | implemented by validation; run in this pass |
| Source-note backlog audit | Check which assigned sources still lack usable source notes. | source notes currently exist for assigned sources; claim mapping remains incomplete |
| New-paper triage scenario | Check whether a new paper updates an existing chapter or creates a new boundary. | planned; not run |

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:bibliography.plan.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.BibliographyPlan` | A source-derived claim requires a source note or equivalent ingested-source artifact. | planned |
| `lean:bibliography.plan.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.BibliographyPlan` | A new source cannot be assigned to a non-existent chapter id. | planned |

## Source crosswalk

This chapter intentionally keeps a broad source set because it governs the backlog for the whole book. Appendix G remains the generated corpus map. Appendix H remains author-intent and lineage context, not evidence.

Priority backlog:

- Claim-to-source mapping for Appendix C.
- External literature normalization and direct citation checks.
- Reproduction or import of any benchmark artifacts before support promotion.
- Lean triage for proof targets that are currently schema or process contracts.
- New-paper triage rules for dynamic chapter insertion.

Recovery boundaries:

- MoECOT, Road To AGI, VCM editable, and CoilMoECOT are readable through connector/source notes, but reported benchmark or runtime claims remain unpromoted.
- Private empirical results must stay out of public claims until artifact, command, environment, and publication permission are recorded.
- External references named inside source documents must be verified directly before becoming bibliography citations.

## Summary

The research agenda is the book's backlog discipline. It keeps source ingestion, bibliography, proof targets, experiments, missing evidence, and chapter insertion rules visible so the living book can grow without losing evidence control.
